description_long: This is a robust, square-topped plant pot carved from a single block of stone, featuring a thick rim and a deep, bowl-like cavity. The exterior surface is rough and weathered, with visible chisel marks and a natural, unpolished finish. On one side, there is a low-relief carving of an animal, possibly a bull or ox, which adds a decorative and historical touch to the pot. The other sides are mostly plain, with subtle textural variations and some minor surface irregularities. The pot appears heavy and solid, suitable for outdoor use in public or garden settings. Its substantial size and weight suggest it is designed to hold medium-sized plants or small shrubs.
description: A heavy, square-topped stone plant pot with a rough finish and an animal relief carving on one side. Its thick walls and deep cavity make it ideal for medium plants in outdoor settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the animal relief carving and the thick, square rim.
description_view_1: Side view with a plain, rough surface and no visible carvings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a mostly unadorned, textured stone surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with another angle of the animal relief and the pot's deep cavity.
attribute_placement_context: commonly used as a decorative planter in public parks or historical gardens
